input:
It was the habit of the people of Makkah to have in their homes idols which they would touch whenever they went out or came back, seeking their blessings. The people of Makkah carried out that order and all idols were broken. Hind bint ʿUtbah took an axe with which to break her idol. As she did so, she said: “We were deceived by you.” The Prophet also sent several expeditions to the surrounding area to remove and break all the idols.
Source: Adil Salahi, Muhammad: Man and Prophet, Ch. 35: A Conquest of Hearts

output.source_reference: Adil Salahi, Muhammad: Man and Prophet, Ch. 35: A Conquest of Hearts

characters.name: Hind bint ʿUtbah
characters.appearance: Mid 20s, medium build, fair skin with a smattering of freckles. Wearing a long-sleeved, off-white cotton thobe with intricate silver embroidery at neckline, brown woolen cloak draped over shoulders, headscarf covering dark hair. No makeup or adornments. Expressive, determined look.

characters.name: Prophet Muhammad (PBUH)
characters.appearance: Early 50s, medium build, dignified upright posture. Wearing a simple white thobe with a brown cloak draped over shoulders, off-white turban with tail between shoulder blades. Simple leather sandals. Always shown from behind, face never visible. Distinguished by brown cloak.

characters.name: Makkans
characters.appearance: Mixed group of 20-30 Arabs, various ages, fair to olive skin tones. Wearing off-white thobes, brown woolen cloaks, headscarves. Dressed in modest attire, no adornments.

output.moral_lesson: Believers must stand firm against idolatry and false worship.
output.narrative_text: The people of Makkah were in the habit of worshipping idols in their homes. But the Prophet sent his followers to break all the idols, and even Hind bint ʿUtbah joined in, saying ‘We were deceived by you’ as she smashed her own idol.

compliance.notes: Makkans shown as silhouettes, idols not depicted in detail. Warm and simple scene, suitable for children.

output.image_prompt: Watercolor illustration of a Makkah home in 7th century Arabia. A young woman, Hind bint ʿUtbah, mid-20s, fair skin, brown cloak, off-white thobe, headscarf, determined look, holding an axe in her right hand, standing in front of her idol. A group of silhouetted people, Makkans, in the background. The Prophet, shown from behind, stands at a distance, in white thobe and brown cloak. Warm golden palette, interior of a mud-brick home with clay water jug in the corner. No modern objects.
